food_subgroup: Fast food pasta meal with meat sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Filipino-style sweet spaghetti or Jollibee Spaghetti. FOODON_00002473 (pasta food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Pasta, cooked) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for Filipino-style sweet spaghetti with meat sauce and hotdog slices.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229953004 (Pasta dish) is the closest defensible match. No specific code for Filipino-style spaghetti exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One regular serving of Jollibee Spaghetti is approximately 250 g, comprising a portion of sweet-style spaghetti with meat sauce, hotdog slices, and a cheese topping, as typically served in a Jollibee fast food meal.

nutrient_source.title: Spaghetti with meat sauce, cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 27054) adapted with reference to Jollibee Philippines published nutrition panel and Filipino spaghetti composition literature
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Jollibee Spaghetti is a Filipino-style sweet spaghetti with ground pork, hotdog slices, banana ketchup-based sauce, and cheese. No exact match exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for spaghetti with meat sauce (NDB 27054) and cross-referenced with Jollibee Philippines published nutrition information (Jollibee Foods Corporation nutrition panel, publicly available). The sweet sauce and hotdog components increase sugar and sodium relative to a standard Western meat sauce. Values represent a best-estimate per-100g composition for this product category.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — spaghetti, boiled, with meat sauce (analogous estimate)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jollibee-style Filipino sweet spaghetti. GI of 52 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for spaghetti with meat sauce (GI ~52, low category), which is the closest analogous food. The sweet banana ketchup sauce adds sugar but the pasta matrix dominates GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 51.25) / 100 ≈ 26.7, based on 250 g serving with 20.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1050 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Jollibee Spaghetti due to its high sodium content (~1050 mg per regular serving), which represents approximately 46% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; portion control and reduced-frequency consumption are strongly advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (52) and high glycemic load per serving (~26.7) due to refined pasta and sweet sauce
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with Jollibee Spaghetti as the high glycemic load per serving (~26.7) from refined pasta and a sugar-enriched banana ketchup sauce can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; smaller portions and pairing with low-GI foods or fibre-rich vegetables are recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium, saturated fat from processed meat (hotdog), and refined carbohydrate content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, Jollibee Spaghetti poses moderate risk due to its combination of high sodium (~1050 mg per serving), saturated fat from processed hotdog slices and meat sauce (~4.75 g per serving), and refined carbohydrates; occasional consumption in small portions is preferable, and regular intake should be avoided as part of a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jollibee Spaghetti Meal is a signature fast food pasta dish served by Jollibee, a Filipino quick-service restaurant chain with outlets across Southeast Asia including Singapore (Bugis Village). It is a distinctly Filipino-style spaghetti that differs markedly from Italian spaghetti: the sauce is sweet rather than savoury, made with a tomato and banana ketchup base, ground pork, and sliced hotdog pieces, topped with grated cheese. The noodles are boiled spaghetti strands coated in this sweet, slightly tangy meat sauce. The flavour profile is sweet, mildly savoury, and umami-rich, with a soft noodle texture and a saucy consistency. Culturally, Jollibee Spaghetti is an iconic comfort food in the Philippines, often associated with birthday celebrations and family gatherings. In Singapore, it is served as a meal set (Spaghetti Meal D) at approximately SGD 8.00. Nutritionally, it is a moderate-energy dish (~370 kcal per 250 g serving) with a significant sodium load (~1050 mg per serving) and a moderate glycemic load (~26.7 per serving) driven by refined pasta and the sweet sauce. It is Halal-certified at the Singapore outlet. Key clinical considerations include high sodium content relevant to hypertension management, moderate glycemic load relevant to blood glucose control in Type 2 Diabetes, and saturated fat from processed meat relevant to cardiovascular health. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those on gluten-free or low-sodium diets.
common_user_queries: Is Jollibee spaghetti halal in Singapore?
How many calories in Jollibee spaghetti?
Is Jollibee spaghetti good for diabetics?
How much sodium in Jollibee spaghetti?
What is the GI of spaghetti with meat sauce?

confidence_reason: No exact match for Jollibee Spaghetti exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy spaghetti with meat sauce and cross-referenced with Jollibee Philippines publicly available nutrition panel data. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for spaghetti with meat sauce, and Jollibee Philippines nutrition panel does not disclose vitamin D. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy composition for spaghetti with meat sauce. The sweet banana ketchup sauce used by Jollibee increases sugar and sodium relative to a standard Western meat sauce; sodium and sugar values are adjusted upward accordingly. This record represents the Jollibee Spaghetti Meal (D) as served at Jollibee Bugis Village, Singapore, at SGD 8.00. The Halal tag is consistent with Jollibee Singapore's Halal certification. Glycemic load per serving = (52 × 20.5 × 2.5) / 100 = 26.65, rounded to 26.7. All -1 sentinel values reflect genuine absence of data in the approved source, not a data entry omission.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft built. Identified that multilingual names needed to be added for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as the food is served in Singapore (Fast Food cuisine, Singapore outlet). Added all required local_names and aliases_by_language entries.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases consistency — all aliases_by_language string values confirmed present verbatim in flat aliases list. Verified all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Verified GI is numeric > 0. Verified ontology match_type values are only 'exact', 'closest_parent', or 'none'. Confirmed all hard gates pass and score is 8/8.
